enonce.texte:
Match the money-related words with their correct definitions.

1. Coin
2. Note
3. Price
4. Cost
5. Bill
6. Amount
Definitions:
A. A piece of paper money
B. The amount of money needed to buy something
C. A small metal piece of money
D. A document showing how much you must pay
E. The total sum of money
F. The value or price of something

questions.texte: Match each word with the correct definition by writing the correct letter (A-F) in your notebook.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct demonstrative pronoun: this, that, these, those

questions.texte: ______ is my book. (near the speaker)
______ are my pens. (near the speaker)
______ is a shop. (far from the speaker)
______ are oranges. (far from the speaker)
______ is my mother. (near the speaker)
______ are my shoes. (far from the speaker)

enonce.texte: Choose the correct question to ask about the price of an item.

questions.texte: How do you ask the price of one apple?
How do you ask the price of several oranges?
How do you ask the price of a shirt?
How do you ask the price of some bananas?

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue and answer the questions.

At the Market

Seller: Good morning! How can I help you?

Customer: Good morning! How much is this bag?

Seller: This bag is 2,000 CFA francs.

Customer: And how much are these shoes?

Seller: Those shoes are 5,000 CFA francs.

Customer: What about this hat?

Seller: That hat is 1,500 CFA francs.

Customer: I will take the bag and the hat. How much is that together?

Seller: That is 3,500 CFA francs.

Customer: Here is 4,000 CFA francs.

Seller: Thank you! Here is your change: 500 CFA francs.

questions.texte: What is the price of the bag?
How much are the shoes?
What is the price of the hat?
How much did the customer pay in total?
questions.reponse: The customer paid 3,500 CFA francs for the bag and the hat.

questions.texte: How much change did the customer receive?
questions.reponse: The customer received 500 CFA francs in change.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with this, that, these, or those.

questions.texte: ______ book here is mine. (near)
______ apples over there are very fresh. (far)
Look at ______ beautiful dress here! (near)
______ shoes in the window are very expensive. (far)
______ oranges here are 100 CFA each. (near)
______ car across the street is my father's. (far)

enonce.texte:
Complete the bill with the correct prices and totals.

ItemQuantityPrice per itemTotal
Rice2 kg800 CFA______
Oil1 bottle1,200 CFA______
Sugar1 kg600 CFA______
TOTAL______

questions.texte: Complete the bill by calculating the total for each item and the grand total.

enonce.texte:
Read the shopping list and answer the questions.

Mama's Shopping List

Mama is going to the market. Here is her list:

  • 2 kg of tomatoes - 300 CFA per kg
  • 3 kg of onions - 200 CFA per kg
  • 1 kg of fish - 2,500 CFA
  • 2 bags of rice - 1,000 CFA each
  • 1 bottle of oil - 1,500 CFA

She has 10,000 CFA francs. Can she buy everything?

questions.texte: How much does Mama need to buy 2 kg of tomatoes?
What is the total cost of the onions?
How much does Mama need to buy everything on her list?
questions.reponse:
Tomatoes: 600 CFA
Onions: 600 CFA
Fish: 2,500 CFA
Rice: 2 × 1,000 = 2,000 CFA
Oil: 1,500 CFA
Total: 7,200 CFA

questions.texte: Does Mama have enough money? How much will she have left?
questions.reponse: Yes, Mama has enough money. She will have 10,000 - 7,200 = 2,800 CFA left.

questions.texte: If Mama buys an extra 2 kg of sugar at 500 CFA per kg, can she still pay?
questions.reponse: Sugar: 2 × 500 = 1,000 CFA. New total: 7,200 + 1,000 = 8,200 CFA. She still has 10,000 CFA, so yes, she can pay. She will have 1,800 CFA left.

enonce.texte:
Write a short dialogue (about 8-10 lines) between a customer and a seller at a market.

The customer wants to buy:

  • 2 bananas
  • 3 oranges
  • 1 bag of rice

Use questions like "How much is...?" and "How much are...?"

questions.texte: Write a dialogue between a customer and a seller.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Customer: Good morning! How are you?

Seller: Good morning! I am fine, thank you. How can I help you?

Customer: How much are these bananas?

Seller: These bananas are 100 CFA each.

Customer: I will take two bananas. How much are those oranges?

Seller: Those oranges are 150 CFA each.

Customer: I will take three oranges. How much is this bag of rice?

Seller: That bag of rice is 1,000 CFA.

Customer: OK. How much is that in total?

Seller: That is 2 bananas (200 CFA) + 3 oranges (450 CFA) + rice (1,000 CFA) = 1,650 CFA.

Customer: Here is 2,000 CFA.

Seller: Thank you! Here is your change: 350 CFA.

enonce.texte: Complete the questions with How much or How many.

questions.texte: ______ apples do you want?
______ water do you drink every day?
______ rice do you need?
______ oranges are in the basket?
______ money do you have?
______ students are in your class?

enonce.texte:
Read the price tags and answer the questions.

Price list:

  • Apple: 200 CFA
  • Orange: 150 CFA
  • Banana: 100 CFA
  • Bread: 500 CFA
  • Milk: 800 CFA

questions.texte: How much is one apple?
How much are three oranges?
What is more expensive: 4 bananas or 1 bread?
questions.reponse: 4 bananas cost 4 × 100 = 400 CFA. Bread costs 500 CFA. The bread is more expensive.

questions.texte: If you buy 2 apples and 1 milk, how much do you pay?
questions.reponse: 2 apples = 2 × 200 = 400 CFA. Milk = 800 CFA. Total = 400 + 800 = 1,200 CFA.

questions.texte: You have 1,000 CFA. What can you buy? Give two different combinations.
questions.reponse:
Possible combinations:
1) 3 apples (600 CFA) + 2 bananas (200 CFA) + 2 oranges (300 CFA) = 1,100 CFA (too much).
2) 5 bananas (500 CFA) + 1 bread (500 CFA) = 1,000 CFA exactly.
3) 4 oranges (600 CFA) + 4 bananas (400 CFA) = 1,000 CFA exactly.

enonce.texte:
Read the receipt and answer the questions.

SUPERMARKET RECEIPT

--------------------

Item: Rice      2 kg      800 CFA/kg      1,600 CFA

Item: Sugar     1 kg      600 CFA/kg      600 CFA

Item: Oil       1 L      1,200 CFA/L      1,200 CFA

Item: Milk      2 L      800 CFA/L      1,600 CFA

--------------------

Subtotal: 5,000 CFA

Tax: 10% (500 CFA)

Total: 5,500 CFA

questions.texte: How much does 2 kg of rice cost?
What is the price of 1 L of oil?
How much did the customer buy milk?
questions.reponse: The customer bought 2 L of milk.

questions.texte: What is the tax amount on this receipt?
If the customer paid with 6,000 CFA, how much change did they receive?
questions.reponse: The total is 5,500 CFA. 6,000 - 5,500 = 500 CFA in change.

Item: Fish         1 kg    ___ CFA/kg    2,500 CFA

Item: Rice        2 kg    1,000 CFA/kg    ___ CFA

--------------------

Total: 5,700 CFA

questions.texte: Complete the receipt by finding the missing values.

questions.texte: Match each amount with its correct written form.

enonce.texte:
You have 5,000 CFA francs. Create a shopping list of at least 5 different items that you can buy at the market.

Use these prices:

  • Bananas: 100 CFA each
  • Oranges: 150 CFA each
  • Tomatoes: 300 CFA per kg
  • Rice: 1,000 CFA per kg
  • Fish: 2,500 CFA per kg
  • Sugar: 600 CFA per kg
  • Oil: 1,200 CFA per bottle
  • Bread: 500 CFA
  • Milk: 800 CFA per bottle
  • Apples: 200 CFA each

Calculate the total cost of your items and show your working.

questions.texte: Create a shopping list of at least 5 items and calculate the total cost.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Shopping list:

  • 4 bananas: 4 × 100 = 400 CFA
  • 3 oranges: 3 × 150 = 450 CFA
  • 1 kg rice: 1,000 CFA
  • 1 kg sugar: 600 CFA
  • 1 bread: 500 CFA
  • 1 L milk: 800 CFA

Total: 400 + 450 + 1,000 + 600 + 500 + 800 = 3,750 CFA

I have 5,000 CFA. I have 1,250 CFA left.
